key_insight: The paper gives a unified, elementary proof strategy for mean-square and sub-Gaussian concentration bounds in contractive stochastic approximation with multiplicative noise and potentially unbounded iterates.
engineering_relevance: This matters for reinforcement learning and other iterative optimization/control systems where contractive operators and state-dependent noise are common, especially under \ell_\infty-style norms.
method: Uses averaged noise and auxiliary iterates to derive a one-step Lyapunov drift inequality directly on the normed error.
Avoids smoothing the norm with generalized Moreau envelopes and skips the multi-stage bootstrapping used in prior concentration proofs.
Uses induction for mean-square control and probabilistic induction over "good" events to enable Azuma-Hoeffding concentration.
evidence_signal: The abstract states theoretical results only, including the first sub-Gaussian tailed maximal concentration bound for SA under multiplicative noise, rather than empirical evaluation.
watch_item: The concentration result depends on a stepsize tuned logarithmically with the confidence level, which may complicate practical parameter selection.
paper_title: Concentration and Mean-Square Bounds for Contractive Stochastic Approximation: A Unified Elementary Approach
